WIS: +3 INT: +3
Focus: Affliction Efficiency I
WT: 0.4 Size: TINY

Race: ALL

Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Quest Item
Slot: WRIST
AC: 2
WIS: +3 INT: +3
Focus: Affliction Efficiency I

▲ Page top
great for shammies too
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2002 at 3:18 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591781198752
23 posts
I'm in highhold right now and I'm thinking whether I should try and get one of these. I'm a lvl 21 shammie and our dot spells are all over 6 ticks. I would use affliction more if it was more mana efficent mobs don't tend to last 21 ticks unless you kite or root. Affliction's our lvl 19 spell and it's 30/6/21. I have bone bracers with extended affliction I which rocks at my lvl for tainted breath when soloing. I am usually about to kill mob when it runs out now. That's a lvl 14 shammie spell and it's 10/8/7. I just wish pbb had more ac as I do melee somewhat. I like the +3 wis too I just wonder if my totemic would be better as it's a dex buff and higher ac. Dex is also supposed to help with casting. It's good for melee too. Two of these would give me like 30 more mana enough for an extra Tainted Breath or gate. I think I would look silly with one of these and one totemic, but hey people would only notice if they inspect. Any shammies out there have any thoughts? I agree with druid post soon i won't need it. I could be to lvl 24 in highhold by the time I get one of these at that point I get better 7 tick spell. true I have alot of duties besides dot, debuff, heal, buff, melee and so on. Mobs at higher lvls resist the lower lvl spells more though. Totemic wrists require you buy 153 pp dufernite though and are harder to get than these. Any thoughts? Anybody know what you need for resists for blindness? If it's magic I should bust out my shiny brass shield to take this on and put away the +9 wisdom shield I got. My screen went blank the last time i tried to get one of these and I thought my computer had ****** up and I turned it off. lol.
▲ Page top
Ineffective Effect (for Wizards)
QuoteReply
# Jul 16 2002 at 11:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250928198
View User Forum
Sage
208 posts
On the surface, this sounds like a nice effect. However, if you're a wizard, the effect is pretty much useless. It's only good on spells level 20 or below, and then only on spells with a duration longer than six ticks. Since most wizard's nukes are going to be an "instant" duration, this effect does nothing.

The only sub-20th spell a wizard can cast that this will help with is the level 12 "Firestorm", which does a whopping 28 points of damage per mob per wave.

Raising the spell cap to 24 for this would be nice, at least then it would affect a spell a wizard is likely to actually use. (Lightning Storm, 75pts/mob/wave.)

So, don't look to this item for the focus effect if you're a wizard. Your only real benefit will be the +3 to INT. (Which is, in fact, a rather nice bump, especially if you have two of these.)

Edited, Tue Jul 16 12:06:16 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Ineffective Effect (for Wizards)
QuoteReply

__DEL__1592791840126

6,424 posts
Root and Instill are affected.

My friend and I (Level 27 wizard and 22 wizard) camped the Lords and EEs in gorge of king xorbb for about three hours and got 2 to drop. we also got 4 hoops and a handful of optic nerves. We had no problems with absense of tank because the eyes and lords have low hp and dont last long against wizzy nukes. But then again it was a pain when we were charmed into nuking the crap out of each other =/

In response to the post that says, "What, at 37 you can't use a WIS 3/ INT 3 braclet?". No, at 37 a Druid should have at least Chipped Bone Braclets. WIS 5. They Drop off of the Ogre's in WK. Nope - went online today & solo'd Lord Syrkl - Stun/Might to try to stop the Mes & blast the Eye with Wrath. It droppped the polished bone bracer, a mithril earring & an Amber.

# Jul 12 2002 at 8:13 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591777513646
View User Forum
Sage
246 posts
This is totaly wrong. It has not been nerfed. In a 3 hour camp I was able to pick up 5 of the PBB's and one PBH (polished bone hoop) off the EE's and Lords. If I had not lost the roll on Lord Soptyvr, I would have had 6 (still conned dk blue to me at lvl 37 so a few kind people helped out). I went back the next day and it was a bit slower but I did get one PBB with in an hour's time.

So if you need a few of these on Ayonne Ro send me a tell.

RE: Nurfed
QuoteReply
# Jul 03 2002 at 5:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773214131
4 posts
Went to pick up some PBB's for an easy int boost. The drop rate does not seem to be nurfed to me, as they dropped 3 in the span of about 2-3 hours. Seems to me that's about the same as it has always been. The drops do tend to be streaky from my experience: 2 drops in 20 minutes, 2 hours without one. Dropped the big cheese and he didn't have one but one Lord and one regular EE had one, don't remember which type dropped the third PBB. Hope that you have better luck finding some next time you're in there.

Thsi item is VERy nice for Necros
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2002 at 12:41 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592793169098
143 posts
Why? simple. Look at necros spells list. You get Heat Blood at 12 and Heart Flutter at 16, and you DO NOT get any other DOT untill 29!!!

so you will be keep using same 2 dots for 8 levels above 20, so this is where this bracelets comes as VERY helpfull

PBB's
QuoteReply
# May 13 2002 at 11:34 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253751719
87 posts
These are still nice items for lower to mid-level players, and a relatively easier item to farm for once someone gets into their 30's. The gems they drop also fetch a few pp, and are stackable. I usually keep a supply of these for the newer guild members, as it makes a nice addition to their equipment.

Evil Eye Drop
QuoteReply
# Dec 04 2001 at 1:41 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757758080
5 posts
I looted this off an Evil Eye in East Karana on Sunday. I killed three in a row,they spawned within five minutes of each other. I spent 9 hour's each of the previous three day's without seeing one Eye. Luck of the draw I guess. Good Luck All.........
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 02 2001 at 11:26 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I got mine today on GM event on first kill guess i got lucky me and my whole group got something we both needed =)
Ok, One more Evil Eye then I'm leaving...Yippee!
QuoteReply
# Oct 30 2001 at 1:45 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1636935661588
126 posts
After a few hours of on and off Gorge camping (over the course of a week), I zoned to RunnyEye and tried hunting Gobbos for their Black Alloy/Iron Armour. I got about half a suit over the course of a few days. My latest group buddy was PBB farming but then the top EE spawned and he wasn't sure he could handle him on his own. I invited him into RE and we tried grouping but kept getting in over our heads. Eventually he got tired of aggroeing everything but the kitchen sink so he left for the Gorge and I continued hunting RE.
After a few minutes, he calls me back saying the Big Cheese EE's still there and would I help him kill it. I didn't want to give up RE so easily, but my pet spontaneously attacked a couple of Foeslicers and got himself dispelled so I figured it was a sign from the EQ gods that I should leave. 'What the hey', I thought. I could still hunt Evil Eyes with him and get a small amount of XP.

Well, I get there and, sure enough, there's the two Lords and the Big Cheese. I told him that I'm LM and it'll take a few minutes for me to med and get a pet. So we sit and wait. Shortly after that a higher level Barbarian walks up and asks if we mind him killing the Big Cheese for us? Great! Saves us trouble, but now I'm a little perturbed that I passed up hunting RE for this. Anyway, he kills off the Evil Eye and my groupmate goes LD before he's finished. I loot the body and get an EE Optic Nerve (Crafted Armour Quest, sweet!) and the lens (evil Cleric quest, blah!). I loot the nerve just in case my buddy comes back and start camping the Evil Eyes. It's boring work, but I still hold out for a chance at picking up a PBB.
Eventually, I run out of inventory space and getting tired of farming Evil Eyes for their junk. 'One more Eye before I log,' I think,'and then I'm heading back to the real world.' So an Evil Eye pops…Fire Bolt thrice…loot body…Hey! A Polished Bone Bracelet! I destroy a Silver Amulet and toss the bracelet in a bag.

My group buddy never did get back online. Mercado, send me a tell if you still want that Optic Nerve that dropped off the Evil Eye.


Cya,
Coddington
Mage 31
▲ Page top
RE: Ok, One more Evil Eye then I'm leaving...Yippee!

i have only gotten 2 of these my whole eq life till last night. I go there alot because theres never anyone there and the money is great, anyhow i got one on the second kill, then another right afterwords on a reg old eye, then went to e karana to sell came back and got another on the second kill :) i was very happy to say the least hehe, sold one for 75 the other two for 100 for the pair....she really needed em )
